title: GAIA .gaia/local state registry
description: Constrains .gaia/state-registry.json: the one classification of every .gaia/local entry (SPEC-061). A malformed edit fails this cheap validation before any consumer or conformance check runs.

id.description: Stable kebab-case key, referenced by consumers/tests.

path.description: Relative to .gaia/local/. May join more than one concrete on-disk shape with '|' when one logical entry covers several filenames (e.g. a clearance marker's .ok/.refused/.dispositions.json siblings); each '|'-joined alternative is matched independently under this entry's own `match` rule. May carry a '<placeholder>' token for a variable path segment (a spec id, a digest, a worktree name): for `match: glob` a placeholder behaves as a shell '*' wildcard, and for `match: prefix` only the literal text before the first '<' is significant (or the whole trimmed path, when it carries no placeholder).

match.description: How `path` matches a real .gaia/local child. exact: literal equality per '|' alternative. glob: shell-glob equality ('*' matches any run of characters, including '/'). prefix: the child's path starts with `path`'s literal text (trimmed of a trailing '/', truncated at its first '<' placeholder if any).

keyed_by.description: Required non-null partition key, lock, or singleton-overwrite strategy when scope=='shared'; null for every other scope. Per-tree's key is the resolver-supplied tree identity, uniform across every per-tree entry and stated once in prose rather than per row; ephemeral's key is its own session/spec/uuid embedded in `path`; main-only is a singleton or a main-anchored coordination store.

why.description: Plain-language reason this entry carries this scope, grounded in the classification's record.

writer.description: code: a shipped writer exists. hand-authored: GAIA-recognized and protected, but a human writes it, never GAIA. not-yet-live: allowlisted for a writer that has not shipped yet. none-residue: never used inside `entries` (residue-only; see residueEntry).

reaped_by.description: The janitor arm that owns this entry's lifecycle, or null when nothing reaps it (a persistent value, or a producer that self-prunes).

source.description: Provenance pointer back to the classification row this entry transcribes.
